channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Caribbean nation of St Kitts launches investigation after 19 people found dead in drifting vessel
item.description: Authorities in the Caribbean nation of St. Kitts and Nevis have launched an investigation after discovering a partially submerged boat containing at least 19 bodies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Panama's president says there will be no negotiation about ownership of canal
item.description: Panama President José Raúl Mulino says there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Alma Solís, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France hands over last military base in Chad
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — France handed over its last military base in Chad to local authorities on Thursday, two months after the former French colony in central Africa broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: MOUTA ALI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European Central bank cuts benchmark rate by a quarter percentage point to boost stagnant economy
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — The European Central Bank cut its key interest rate on Thursday to boost an economy that’s struggling to grow as consumers burned by inflation warily eye price tags and businesses try to navigate political turmoil in leading economies France and Germany.
item.dc:creator: DAVID McHUGH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: More schools in France are getting special mailboxes for children to flag abuse
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A special mailbox for children to flag abuses was set up Thursday at a Paris primary school, a first in the French capital as part of nationwide effort to encourage more victims of sexual violence to speak up.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Russian drone strikes an apartment building in Ukraine, killing at least 6
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian drone blasted a hole in an apartment building in northeastern Ukraine during a nighttime attack, killing at least six people and wounding nine others, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Albanians pay last respects to Orthodox Christian Archbishop Anastasios
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Thousands of Albanians gathered in the capital on Thursday to pay their last respects to Archbishop Anastasios, who revived the country’s Orthodox Christian Church after the fall of the Communist party in 1990.
item.dc:creator: BY LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: European court finds Italy violated the right to life of Italians living in Naples toxic dump area
item.description: ROME (AP) — The European Court of Human Rights ruled Thursday that Italy violated the right to life of Italians living in a toxic-waste polluted area around Naples and ordered the government to develop a strategy to address and monitor the contamination and resulting health problems.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo say they plan to take their fight to the capital
item.description: Rwanda-backed rebels who have captured eastern Congo’s largest city say they plan to take their rebellion to the capital of Kinshasa and seek to gain political power.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Third round of hostage releases begins as part of Hamas' Gaza ceasefire agreement with Israel
item.description: Hamas released eight more hostages in Gaza Thursday as part of an ongoing ceasefire agreement with Israel in exchange for releasing hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International Criminal Court has Putin, Netanyahu in its sights, yet its courtrooms are empty
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — For a few hours last week, the International Criminal Court looked poised to take a Libyan warlord into custody. Instead, member state Italy sent the head of a notorious network of detention centers back home.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia comes in from the cold, with flowers blooming around Moscow in January
item.description: January in Russia conjures up images of Muscovites crunching through the snow in bulky coats -- not bunches of delicate snowdrops blooming in grassy areas around still-standing Christmas trees.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HARRIET MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans urge Trump to sanction ICC after failed legislation push
item.description: Senate Republicans are recommending President Trump issue an executive order to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC) for its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel, after Senate Democrats blocked sanctions legislation absent a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mexico's president to send Google letter over Gulf of America change
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday the country will send Google a letter asking about the technology giant's decision to soon change the name of the Gulf of Mexico to Gulf of America. "To change the name of an international sea, it is not a country that changes it.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Backlash may shatter President Macron's plans to leave mark on Notre Dame windows
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron’s plans for installing contemporary windows inside the restored Notre Dame Cathedral is facing furious opposition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 2 Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, officials say
item.description: Israel killed two Palestinians in the northern West Bank overnight and into Wednesday, the occupied territory's Heath Ministry said, as the Israeli military focuses on a major crackdown there during the ceasefire in Gaza. There was no immediate comment from the Israel's military.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Hamas hostage details horrors of captivity, credits kidnapped IDF soldier with saving her life
item.description: Former hostage Amit Soussana, who was the first Israeli woman to speak about being sexual assaulted while in Hamas captivity, says kidnapped IDF soldier Liri Albag saved her life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India's Modi speaks with 'dear friend' President Trump amid hopes of furthering ties
item.description: Following a call between President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, India is seeking to boost bilateral ties and trade with the U.S. amid an already friendly relationship between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats block GOP's ICC sanctions bill
item.description: Senate Democrats on Tuesday blocked Republicans from advancing legislation to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC), arguing the bill had sweeping consequences on allies and American businesses.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US consulate warns of gun battles, IEDs, kidnappings in Mexican border towns near Texas
item.description: U.S. officials have put several Mexican border towns near Texas under the highest-level travel advisory amid gun battles, kidnappings and IEDs in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9
